title: Water Properties and Ecological Importance
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive diagram showing five key properties of water with hover-to-reveal ecological connections, a central H2O molecule, and a quiz mode

learningObjectives: Explain five key properties of water and connect each property to its ecological importance
Describe how the molecular structure of water gives rise to these properties
Predict ecological consequences if water lacked any one of these properties
prerequisites: Basic understanding of molecular structure and polarity

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Hover-to-reveal predict-then-discover with self-assessment quiz mode
keyQuestions: How does the polarity of water molecules give rise to its unique properties?
Why is water's density anomaly critical for aquatic life in cold climates?
How do cohesion and adhesion work together to transport water in plants?
What would happen to Earth's climate without water's high heat capacity?
commonMisconceptions: Students often think ice floats because it contains air bubbles, rather than understanding that water expands when it crystallizes into a less dense structure
Students may believe water's solvent properties only apply to dissolving salt, not realizing it dissolves gases, minerals, and organic molecules essential for life
Students sometimes confuse cohesion (water-to-water attraction) with adhesion (water-to-other-surface attraction)
assessmentOpportunities: Use the Quiz Me feature to test property-to-example matching
Have students write explanations connecting molecular structure to each ecological property
Ask students to predict what would happen to specific ecosystems if one water property were removed
